This is the list of options and their score: Friday lunchtime: 10, Monday morning: 10, Saturday night: 10, Sunday night: 10, Wednesday evening: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Monday morning: 10, Sunday night: 10, Friday lunchtime: 10, Wednesday evening: 10, Saturday night: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Earth: 10, Mars: 10, Mercury: 10, Saturn: 10, Venus: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Venus: 10, Earth: 10, Saturn: 10, Mercury: 10, Mars: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Mars: 10, Saturn: 10, Venus: 10, Earth: 10, Mercury: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Ford: 10, Mercedes: 10, Pontiac: 10, Porsche: 10, Volkswagen: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Volkswagen: 10, Pontiac: 10, Porsche: 10, Ford: 10, Mercedes: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: cheese omelette: 10, ham omelette: 10, mushroom omelette: 10, plain omelette: 10, prawn omelette: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: mushroom omelette: 10, prawn omelette: 10, ham omelette: 10, cheese omelette: 10, plain omelette: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: prawn omelette: 10, plain omelette: 10, mushroom omelette: 10, ham omelette: 10, cheese omelette: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: 1: 10, 2: 10, 7: 10, 10: 10, 15: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: deck chair: 10, dining chair: 10, easy chair: 10, rocking chair: 10, stool: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: stool: 10, rocking chair: 10, deck chair: 10, easy chair: 10, dining chair: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: easy chair: 10, stool: 10, dining chair: 10, rocking chair: 10, deck chair: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Florida: 10, Colorado: 10, Nevada: 10, Ontario: 10, Louisiana: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: jogging: 10, sitting: 10, sunbathing: 10, swimming: 10, walking: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: sunbathing: 10, jogging: 10, walking: 10, sitting: 10, swimming: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: walking: 10, swimming: 10, jogging: 10, sunbathing: 10, sitting: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: David: 10, John: 10, Michael: 10, Robert: 10, Steven: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Michael: 10, Steven: 10, John: 10, David: 10, Robert: 10.  - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: win nothing: 10, win trophy: 10, win chocolate: 10, win champagne: 10, win money: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: win money: 10, win champagne: 10, win nothing: 10, win trophy: 10, win chocolate: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: blue: 10, green: 10, orange: 10, purple: 10, red: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: red: 10, purple: 10, blue: 10, orange: 10, green: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: mango juice: 10, grapefruit juice: 10, pineapple juice: 10, apple juice: 10, carrot juice: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: carrot juice: 10, pineapple juice: 10, mango juice: 10, grapefruit juice: 10, apple juice: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Berlin: 10, Calais: 10, Paris: 10, Prague: 10, Rome: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Paris: 10, Berlin: 10, Rome: 10, Calais: 10, Prague: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Rome: 10, Calais: 10, Prague: 10, Berlin: 10, Paris: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. Your earnings are determined by your decisions alone. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.
